title: hieracium maculatum subsp. ausserdorferi
intro: Hieracium maculatum subsp. ausserdorferi, also known as spotted hawkweed, is a rare and endangered subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family. The plant's leaves and roots have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to have medicinal effects on the skin and digestive system. The health benefits of Hieracium maculatum subsp. ausserdorferi include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and support the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, but due to its rarity and potential toxicity, it is essential to consult with a qualified herbalist or healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
